user_message: Give me the manager view on this Woodgrove Labs support ticket.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Robert Petrov <robert.petrov@woodgrovelab.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-17 15:45 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Woodgrove Labs account health

I wanted to follow up on our conversation about account health. Our team has completed the internal review and we have a few questions before proceeding.

---
From: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
To: Robert Petrov <robert.petrov@woodgrovelab.com>
Date: 2024-02-09 14:05 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Woodgrove Labs account health

Just checking in on the account health proposal. Our CFO is asking for a status update before the board meeting next week.

---
From: Robert Petrov <robert.petrov@woodgrovelab.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-16 12:54 EST
Subject: Quarterly Update: Woodgrove Labs account health

Thank you for the discussion last week. We'd like to move forward with account health. Can we schedule a call this week to finalize the timeline?

signals.detail: Signal detected for Woodgrove Labs: there's a compliance requirement we hadn't accounted for in the original scope

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF42-A trap (Treats casual workload mention as commitment); produces complete Signal assessment, opportunity hypothesis with confidence, ownership routing, response draft, next-s
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF42-A failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF42-A failure mode (Treats casual workload mention as commitment)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct account health decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF42-A -- Treats casual workload mention as commitment
